chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُعْتَكِفِ يَمْرَضُ وَ الْمُعْتَكِفَةِ تَطْمَثُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 81 – The one performing Itikaaf becomes sick, and the female performing Itikaaf menstruates

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/435)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/435)
text: إِذَا مَرِضَ الْمُعْتَكِفُ وَ طَمِثَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ الْمُعْتَكِفَةُ فَإِنَّهُ يَأْتِي بَيْتَهُ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُ إِذَا بَرَأَ وَ يَصُومُ .
وَ فِي رِوَايَةٍ أُخْرَى عَنْهُ لَيْسَ عَلَى الْمَرِيضِ ذَلِكَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws  having said: ‘When the male performing Itikaaf becomes sick and the female performing Itikaaf menstruates, so he should go to his house, then return when he is cured and he should Fast’.
And in another report from him<sup>asws</sup>: ‘That is not upon the sick one’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn ‘Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from Safwan ibn Yahya from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one in the state of Ttikaf becomes ill or a female begins to experience Hayd (menses); they can go home then after getting well perform the ’ Ttikaf again and fast.’”
